Alright, we're at the final step, which is putting it all together. So you're going to assemble the steps, you're going to read it aloud to yourself, and to hopefully you'll have somebody you can read it to to make sure it's very conversational. You're going to know where the testable parts are, things that will change as you test in the market. And then you're going to let it fly out into the world. The final assembly is actually pretty straightforward. Imagine your copy is a real conversation with a single prospect. But when you read it aloud, this is all you can say in the conversation. Everything has to be there. You're taking nothing for granted, you're not thinking, I would add this if I was actually talking to someone. Nope, that has to go into the actual sales message you have. It must be coherent, it must make sense, it must flow just like a real conversation. Now let's take the basic steps here. You learned these steps in the rational, logical order that I in my career would go through the steps myself. These steps were not necessarily the way they would appear in the sales message. So this is how we're going to take the steps and show you where they actually go in the final assembly. So in the high, here's who I am part of the four-step segment, high here's who I am, here's what I have for you, here's what it does for you, here's what you need to do now. The first step, high here's who I am, you insert your story for immediate credibility, your story of course is step number nine. And your USP comes into play here, step number three. Now you're flavoring everything along the way generously with testimonials, step number six. See how other people, if you're online, see how other people use testimonials either to break up copy, to be a certain segment in a video, whether they run along columns on either side of everything, whether they're in a bunch, whether they're spread out, find out how other people are doing it, find the way you want to do it, but that would be step six, put in here flavored generously as I like to say. In the here's what I have for you, you insert your bullets, step number five, based on your features and benefits, step number four, then here's why this is so critical for you, or here's what this will do for you, that's salesmanship, step number eight. Then here's what you need to do now, you insert your sales funnel, step number eleven, you continue through steps twelve to seventeen until you've included all the closing tactics in your arsenal. You write to your avatar, step number two, using your voice, step number nine again. You use hooks to grab your prospects attention, step number seven, using all of your market research to understand what makes him tick, step number one. You create headlines to start the process, step number ten, and armed with all of this information and insight, you cannot help but deliver the most exciting conversation he's had all day and maybe all of his life. We've learned all of this in the order we did because that's the logical way to put together your arsenal of info, insight, and strategies. This is the exact formula that I've used for thirty years and I've taught the thousands of students. Now, it's your turn, go through all the steps each time you write until it becomes automatic. And for emails and short pitches, you can leave info out but you must know what you're leaving out and have a reason for doing so. This gets rid of writer's block by the way. Writer's block is merely not knowing what to write when you sit down to write. And I've been telling you over and over again, don't sit down to write until you've done the basic building blocks of putting the pitch together. So by the time you sit down to write now, you're not going to be stuck because you have your bullets out, you've done your research, you understand who you're writing to, you understand what kind of salesmanship you're going to perform to go through the funnel. Everything is in order. So now you're just sitting down and putting the pieces together. You can map out a sales strategy to approach any market with any product or service and overcome any obstacle to making the sale. Now go get them and that's all folks. All right and for your final exercise, go ahead and do a draft and put it all together and see what you've got.
